member.name: Stuart Anderson
member.synopsis: Stuart Anderson is the Conservative MP for South Shropshire , and has been an MP continually since 12 December 2019.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Broadband: South Shropshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment she has made of the adequacy of the quality of mobile phone connections in South Shropshire.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Government’s ambition is for all populated areas to have access to higher quality standalone 5G by 2030; this is a UK wide ambition.In Ofcom’s Connected Nations Spring update, published on 13 May 2026, it is reported that, as of January 2026, standalone 5...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Armed Forces: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what steps he is taking to improve the survivability of the armed forces.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is taking a broad programme of action to improve the readiness, lethality, and survivability of the Armed Forces. Through the Defence Investment Plan (DIP), we are investing £298 billion over the next four years to rebuild warfighting readi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Horse Racing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what steps she is taking to support the horse racing industry.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the significant contribution that racing makes to the nation’s economy and sporting landscape.Horseracing is the only sport in receipt of a direct government-mandated levy for the purposes of supporting horse breeds, advancing ve...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Broadband: South Shropshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, how many full-fibre connections have been made in South Shropshire constituency in the last 12 months.
writtenQuestions.answerText: According to the Ofcom Connected Nations, full-fibre coverage was delivered to 3,586 additional premises in South Shropshire in the 12 months between January 2025 and January 2026. This increased full fibre coverage from approximately 55% (26,274) to 62% ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Community Ownership Fund: South Shropshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what steps he is taking to ensure that the Community Ownership Fund reaches communities in South Shropshire.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Community Ownership Fund has helped communities across the United Kingdom to take ownership of assets that are important to their local areas. Since 2021, the Fund has awarded over £135 million to 410 projects across the UK, including over £1.8 millio...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Visitor Levy: South Shropshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of an Overnight Visitor Levy on tourist numbers in South Shropshire.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The precise design and scope of the power to introduce an overnight visitor levy are still being developed. It will be for Mayors to decide whether a levy is right for their area, subject to local consultation on specific proposals to understand local imp...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Economic Situation: Shropshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of trends in the level of economic productivity in Shropshire since July 2024.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Office for National Statistics publishes regional productivity statistics annually. The latest data, published in 2025, cover the period up to 2023. Data for 2024 are not yet available.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Public Sector: Food
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what percentage of food procured in the public sector is produced domestically.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Data collection on the extent to which public sector settings are serving food from local sustainable sources is currently underway. We expect the work to be completed by Summer 2027. The Government is open to considering all lawful means of achieving its...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Veterans: Rural Areas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what steps he is taking to improve access to support services for veterans in rural areas.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This government is committed to ensuring that veterans and their families are easily able to access support across the country when and where it is needed, including rural or remote areas. Last year we launched a new Veterans Strategy which recognises vet...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Postal Workers: South Shropshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps he is taking to increase the number of postal workers in South Shropshire.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As Royal Mail is an independent business, the government is not involved in its operational matters including decisions around recruitment and workforce levels.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Courts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what steps his Department is taking to reduce the court backlogs.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government has already invested significantly in the system – in uncapped sitting days for 2026/27, court buildings and technology, and in legal professionals with significant investment in legal aid. Only by pulling every lever we have – investment,...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: Agriculture: Countryside
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what action is being taken to support farming in protected landscapes.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As a result of the 2025 Spending Review, Defra secured a strong financial settlement. The Government has allocated a record £11.8 billion to sustainable farming and food production over this parliament. This includes funding for the Environmental Land Man...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Omaveloxolone
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to ensure patients diagnosed with Friedrich’s Ataxia can access Omavelexolone through the NHS.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE) is the independent body responsible for developing evidence-based recommendations for the National Health Service on whether new, licensed medicines represent a clinically and cost-effective use...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Hill Farming: South Shropshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps her Department is taking to support upland farmers in South Shropshire constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Earlier this year, the Government extended the Farming in Protected Landscapes programme for another three years. It includes support for upland farmers and land managers in South Shropshire’s Shropshire Hills National Landscape. Defra’s Environmental Lan...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Roads: South Shropshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what steps her Department is taking to prevent potholes in South Shropshire constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has confirmed a record £7.3 billion investment into local highways maintenance over the next four years. By confirming funding allocations for the next four-year period, authorities have certainty to plan ahead and shift from short-term fix...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Energy: Rural Areas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps he is taking to help reduce energy costs for rural businesses.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are closely monitoring the impact of energy prices on businesses caused by the instability in the Middle East, and much will depend on the length of the ongoing crisis. We are increasing the Boiler Upgrade Scheme (BUS) grant for properties heated by oi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Domestic Abuse: Rural Areas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what action she is taking to help improve the availability of domestic abuse services in rural areas.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Every victim of violence against women and girls (VAWG), whether in a city or a rural village, should be able to access the help they need. In the Violence Against Women and Girls (VAWG) Strategy, we committed to reform how victim’s services are commissio...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Productivity: Rural Areas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what fiscal steps her Department is taking to increase rural productivity.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government allocated a record amount to sustainable farming and food production over this Parliament at the Spending Review 2025. This will boost productivity and protect the natural ecosystems underpinning food production and broader economy activity...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury
